A fun evening of games, snacks, and fellowship for teens and young adults. Bring a friend!

Join us for a Valentine's-themed game night! We'll have board games, video games, trivia, and plenty of food. This is a great event to invite friends who haven't visited Living Water yet.

**What to bring:** A snack to share and a friend!

Serve our local community together! We'll be helping at the Springfield Food Bank and neighborhood cleanup.

This is a great opportunity to put our faith into action and serve our neighbors. Community service hours will be documented for students who need them.

**Wear:** Comfortable clothes and closed-toe shoes.
**Lunch provided** after the service project.

Our annual spring retreat — a weekend of worship, teaching, outdoor activities, and deep community.

Get away from the routine and spend a weekend growing closer to God and each other. This year's theme is **"Rooted & Growing"** based on Colossians 2:6-7.

### Schedule Highlights

- **Saturday morning:** Worship and session 1
- **Saturday afternoon:** Hiking, team activities, free time
- **Saturday evening:** Bonfire worship and testimonies
- **Sunday morning:** Session 2, small group discussion, and lunch

**Cost:** $45 per person (scholarships available — just ask!)

**Registration deadline:** February 28, 2026

Jesus calls Himself the Good Shepherd who lays down His life for the sheep. What does that mean for how we trust Him?

## Sermon Notes

Jesus' metaphor of the Good Shepherd was deeply personal to His audience — and it speaks just as powerfully to us today.

Sermon Notes

Jesus’ metaphor of the Good Shepherd was deeply personal to His audience — and it speaks just as powerfully to us today.
Identity in Christ: Who Am I?
Exploring what it means to find our true identity not in culture, achievement, or appearance — but in Christ.

## Sermon Notes

Many of us grow up navigating between cultures, expectations, and identities. But Scripture tells us our deepest identity is found in who God says we are.

### Key Points

1. Who we were — dead in trespasses and sins
2. What God did — made us alive together with Christ
3. Who we are — His workmanship, created for good works
4. How we live — walking in the identity He has given us

Jesus invites us to come to Him and drink — what does it mean to receive the living water He offers?

## Sermon Notes

In this message, we explore Jesus' radical invitation at the Feast of Tabernacles. What did it mean for His original audience, and what does it mean for us today?

### Key Points

1. **The Setting** — Understanding the Feast of Tabernacles and why Jesus' words were so powerful
2. **The Invitation** — "If anyone is thirsty, let him come to me and drink"
3. **The Promise** — Rivers of living water flowing from within
4. **The Application** — How we receive and share this living water in our daily lives

A weekly gathering for college students and young professionals. We share a meal, study the Bible, and support each other through life transitions.
Whether you're in college, starting your first job, or figuring out what's next, this group is a place to belong. We rotate between members' homes each week and always start with food!

A midweek Bible study for high school students. We go through books of the Bible together and talk about how faith connects to real life.
This group is designed for 9th-12th graders who want to dig deeper into God's Word. We keep it real, ask hard questions, and support each other through the ups and downs of high school.

An early morning prayer group that intercedes for the church, community, and world. All ages welcome.
We gather every Saturday morning to pray together. No experience needed — just a heart willing to talk to God. We also share prayer requests and celebrate answered prayers.
